MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication ******Re-Noticed*******
Off Calendar Mar-12-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS SHALINI SADDA, DAVID COHEN, CITY REAL ESTATE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

Appearances: Hilary Wild of Lagasse Branch Bell & Kinkead LLP (628.222.5870) for defendants and moving parties; Duncan MacDonald of MacDonald Law (415.956.7777) for plaintiff and responding party.

Argued and the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DENIED. Moving party fails to comply with CCP 1010 and CRC 3.1350(b) ("If summary adjudication is sought, whether separately or as an alternative to the motion for summary judgment, the specific cause of action, affirmative defense, claims for damages, or issues of duty must be stated specifically in the notice of motion and be repeated, verbatim, in the separate statement of undisputed material facts."). Cases cited in the reply for the proposition that the defective notice for motion for summary judgment should be disregarded are inapposite. None of the cited cases found that the defective notice could be disregarded. None of the cited cases suggest that a single mention of "summary judgment on all of the claims" in the "Conclusion" section of the brief saves the defective notice. The Court notes that Summary Judgment is not even mentioned in the "Argument" section of the brief, which consists of subsections dedicated solely to individual causes of action of which summary adjudication is requested (albeit also improperly as issues listed in the Notice of Motion do not match the issues listed in the Separate Statement). Order signed. Judge: Harry L. Jacobs, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Carol Lynn Cox, CSR 5128 (T: 714.336.1430, E: carollynncox@aol.com) (501/HLJ)
